summary: Excellent audience recording of the 9/19/90 show captures a fire‑filled set with soaring Jack Straw, an epic Let It Grow jam and stellar Bruce Hornsby interplay.
review: The tape delivers crystal‑clear audience sound – bass is punchy, guitars sparkle and even listeners who complain about low‑end still hear a well‑balanced mix. Musically the night shines from a ripping Jack Straw opener through a second set highlighted by Let It Grow > Jam, long Franklin’s Tower and inspired Hornsby keyboards. Overall the performance is described as “smokin’”, “magical” and one of the best of the post‑Brent era.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews overwhelmingly praise both the musical performance and the recording quality; show highlights are repeatedly cited and the audience source is praised as exceptional.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with strong bass presence (B&K’s). A few reviewers note a slight lack of low‑end, but overall the sound is superb—often called better than available soundboard versions.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with a high‑energy Jack Straw, builds through tight first‑set work and erupts on an explosive second set featuring a marathon Let It Grow jam, extended Franklin’s Tower and seamless segues (Playing > Ship of Fools > Space > Uncle John's Band). The flow is praised as tight, dynamic and emotionally resonant.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is repeatedly lauded – shredding lead on Jack Straw, top‑notch playing throughout, voice a bit haggard but still powerful.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is highlighted as clear and punchy; many note the B&K’s “grabbed Phil’s bass” for the whole show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s high vocal notes (e.g., in Memphis Blues) and overall contributions receive positive nods.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby’s keyboards earn glowing praise for melodic interplay with Garcia, energizing the band and delivering delicate yet inventive jams.
